name: 8Base
notes: Prolific double-extortion RaaS group active since mid-2022, surging in 2023-2024 with hundreds of victims across SMBs in multiple sectors; uses Phobos ransomware variant.

name: Agrius
notes: Iranian state-sponsored group conducting destructive wiper attacks and data-leak operations against Israeli and Middle Eastern targets; linked to Ministry of Intelligence and Security (MOIS).

name: Akira
notes: Rapidly growing RaaS operation launched in 2023 that became second most-active group in 2025 with 717+ victims; targets Windows and Linux/ESXi environments using double extortion.

name: Andariel
notes: North Korean Lazarus sub-group (Reconnaissance General Bureau) specializing in ransomware deployment and financial theft against defense, healthcare, and financial institutions.

name: Anonymous Sudan
notes: Pro-Russian/pro-Islamic DDoS-for-hire group that conducted high-volume Layer 7 attacks against Western and Israeli targets in 2023-2024; two Sudanese brothers indicted by US DOJ in October 2024.

name: APT-C-36
notes: Latin America-focused cyberespionage group primarily targeting Colombian and Ecuadorian government, finance, and law enforcement entities using commodity RATs delivered via phishing.

name: APT1
notes: PLA Unit 61398 group infamous for large-scale intellectual property theft from US defense, aerospace, and energy firms; publicly indicted in 2014; largely dormant since exposure.

name: APT28
notes: Russian GRU Unit 26165 conducting aggressive espionage and influence operations against NATO governments, defense, and political targets; linked to 2016 US election interference and ongoing European operations.

name: APT29
notes: Russian SVR (Foreign Intelligence Service) conducting high-sophistication supply chain and cloud identity attacks; responsible for SolarWinds compromise, Microsoft Exchange breach in 2024, and ongoing credential theft targeting Western governments.

name: APT32
notes: Vietnamese state-linked group conducting espionage against foreign governments, dissidents, and corporations with interests in Southeast Asia; uses custom malware and living-off-the-land techniques.

name: APT33
notes: Iranian IRGC-linked group targeting aerospace, defense, and energy sectors in the US, Saudi Arabia, and South Korea with spear-phishing and password spray attacks.

name: APT37
notes: North Korean RGB-linked group focused on espionage against South Korean defectors, media, and government using zero-days and mobile malware including RokRAT.

name: APT38
notes: North Korean Lazarus financial crime sub-unit responsible for billions in SWIFT fraud, cryptocurrency heists (including $625M Ronin Network hack), and targeting of banks and crypto exchanges globally.

name: APT40
notes: PLA-linked group conducting maritime and naval espionage targeting defense contractors, research universities, and governments in the Indo-Pacific; exploits edge devices and VPNs for initial access.

name: APT41
notes: Dual-purpose PRC group uniquely conducting both state-sponsored espionage and financially motivated attacks including supply chain compromises; linked to MSS-contracted operators.

name: APT42
notes: IRGC Intelligence Organization sub-group specializing in targeted credential theft and surveillance against political targets, journalists, and dissidents using elaborate social engineering.

name: AvosLocker
notes: RaaS operation active 2021-2023 targeting critical infrastructure including US financial and healthcare sectors; known for using legitimate remote tools like AnyDesk and disabling security software.

name: BianLian
notes: Extortion-only group (shifted from encryption in 2023) targeting professional services, healthcare, and construction via ProxyShell exploitation and custom Go-based tools; sanctioned by CISA.

name: BlackBasta
notes: Sophisticated RaaS group believed to be Conti successor; achieved 500+ victims in two years using Qakbot/QBot for initial access, Cobalt Strike for lateral movement, and double extortion.

name: BlackByte
notes: RaaS group active since 2021 targeting critical US infrastructure including schools and hospitals; deploys custom .NET ransomware and sells victim data tiered by access level.

name: BlackCat
notes: Sophisticated Rust-based RaaS that evolved from DarkSide/BlackMatter; dismantled after FBI operation in December 2023; responsible for Change Healthcare ($22M ransom), MGM, and Caesars attacks.

name: BlackSuit
notes: Rebranded Royal ransomware (itself a Conti successor); active since 2023 targeting healthcare, education, and government with Conti-derived code and high ransom demands ($1M-$10M).

name: Cactus
notes: RaaS group active since 2023 that self-encrypts its binary to evade AV detection; exploits VPN vulnerabilities for initial access and uses double extortion against enterprise targets.

name: Candiru
notes: Israeli mercenary spyware vendor selling DevilsTongue/Sherlock surveillance implants targeting Windows, iOS, and Android; sanctioned by US Commerce Department in November 2021.

name: Cl0p
notes: Russian-speaking RaaS group known for mass zero-day exploitation campaigns targeting file transfer tools (GoAnywhere, MOVEit, Accellion); hundreds of simultaneous victims per campaign wave.

name: Cobalt Group
notes: Russian-speaking group responsible for $1B+ in losses to financial institutions via ATM jackpotting, SWIFT fraud, and card-processing compromise; core members arrested in 2018.

name: Conti
notes: Highly organized Russian RaaS operation and Ryuk successor that attacked 1,000+ organizations; disbanded in May 2022 after internal chat leaks and infighting over Russia-Ukraine war stance; members dispersed to BlackBasta, Hive, and others.

name: CURIUM
notes: IRGC-linked group targeting Middle Eastern defense contractors and US military personnel via elaborate fake social media personas and romance lures to deliver malware.

name: CyberAv3ngers
notes: IRGC-linked group conducting disruptive attacks on Israeli and US water/industrial control systems (Unitronics PLCs); sanctioned by OFAC in 2024.

name: Dark Angels
notes: Big-game hunting ransomware group known for single high-value targets; received a record $75M ransom payment from pharmaceutical firm Cencora in 2024.

name: Darkhotel
notes: South Korean-linked espionage group targeting senior executives and government officials via hotel WiFi networks, spear-phishing, and watering holes across Asia-Pacific.

name: DarkSide
notes: RaaS operated by FIN7/Carbon Spider behind the May 2021 Colonial Pipeline attack; shut down under law enforcement pressure shortly after; evolved into BlackMatter, then BlackCat/ALPHV.

name: Dragonfly
notes: FSB-linked group conducting persistent espionage and pre-positioning operations against Western energy, water, and critical infrastructure; linked to 2017 US power grid compromise campaigns.

name: DragonForce
notes: Ransomware cartel that in 2025 absorbed RansomHub and LockBit affiliates, forming a 'DragonForce Cartel' allowing affiliates to use custom branding; active against global critical infrastructure.

name: Earth Lusca
notes: China-linked group conducting espionage against governments and energy firms in Asia, Africa, and the Middle East using ProxyShell exploits and Cobalt Strike; tracked as a separate cluster from APT41.

name: Embargo
notes: Relatively new RaaS operation (2024) using custom Rust-based encryptor and 'MDeployer' EDR killer tool; primarily delivered by Storm-0501 against US government and critical infrastructure.

name: Ember Bear
notes: GRU-linked unit responsible for WhisperGate wiper attacks against Ukrainian government targets in January 2022; attributed to GRU Unit 161 (formerly known as Sandworm sub-element).

name: Equation Group
notes: NSA-linked elite threat actor behind the Stuxnet co-development, DoubleFantasy/TripleFantasy implants, and EternalBlue exploit; Shadow Brokers leaked toolset in 2017 enabling global ransomware waves.

name: Evil Corp
notes: Sanctioned Russian cybercriminal group led by Maksim Yakubets responsible for Dridex banking trojan, BitPaymer, WastedLocker, Hades, and PhoenixLocker ransomware operations; $100M+ in financial damage.

name: FIN6
notes: Eastern European group specializing in PoS intrusions and Magecart-style e-commerce skimming; pivoted to ransomware deployment using Ryuk and LockerGoga as an affiliate.

name: FIN7
notes: Prolific Russian/Ukrainian e-crime group that stole $1B+ from US hospitality and retail via PoS malware; evolved into ransomware operations as DarkSide/BlackMatter operator and AvNeutralizer tool vendor.

name: FIN8
notes: Financially motivated group targeting payment card data from hospitality, retail, and entertainment sectors; known for long dwell times and selective high-value targeting; recently linked to White Rabbit ransomware.

name: Flax Typhoon
notes: Chinese state-sponsored group targeting Taiwanese government, defense, and technology organizations; operates a large IoT botnet (Raptor Train, 260,000+ devices) for covert data exfiltration.

name: FOG
notes: Ransomware group emerging in 2024 targeting US education and recreation sectors by abusing compromised VPN credentials; deploys customized Fog encryptor against Windows and Linux.

name: Fox Kitten
notes: Iranian IRGC-linked group selling initial access to compromised US and Israeli organizations to ransomware affiliates; exploits VPN and firewall vulnerabilities (Fortinet, Pulse, Citrix) at scale.

name: Gamaredon Group
notes: FSB-affiliated group conducting high-volume spear-phishing campaigns almost exclusively against Ukrainian military, government, and critical infrastructure since 2013.

name: HAFNIUM
notes: Chinese state-sponsored group responsible for the March 2021 Microsoft Exchange Server zero-day exploitation (ProxyLogon) that compromised 250,000+ servers globally.

name: Handala
notes: Pro-Palestinian/Iranian-linked hacktivist group conducting destructive wiper attacks and data-leak extortion against Israeli organizations; MITRE links VOID MANTICORE as the related state-actor component.

name: Helldown
notes: Emerging RaaS group active in 2024-2025 that exploits Zyxel firewall vulnerabilities for initial access; targets SMBs and mid-market companies across Europe and North America.

name: Hunters International
notes: RaaS group that acquired and rebranded Hive ransomware code; shifted in 2025 to extortion-only 'World Leaks' model; maintains aggressive data-theft focus against healthcare and critical sectors.

name: INC Ransom
notes: Active since 2023 targeting healthcare (including NHS Scotland) and public sector organizations with multi-TB data theft and high ransom demands; recently publicly leaked stolen NHS patient data.

name: IntelBroker
notes: Prolific access broker and data extortionist who became BreachForum admin in 2024; responsible for high-profile breaches of Cisco, Europol, Apple, AMD, and US military contractors.

name: Intellexa
notes: EU-headquartered commercial spyware consortium selling Predator/Alien zero-click mobile surveillance platform to repressive governments; sanctioned by US State Department in 2024.

name: Interlock
notes: RaaS group active since late 2024 using ClickFix social engineering to deploy Interlock RAT and ransomware payload; targets healthcare, IT, and government organizations across North America and Europe.

name: Ke3chang
notes: Long-running Chinese MSS-linked group targeting European and Latin American foreign ministries and defense ministries with custom malware families including Okrum and Ketrum.

name: KillNet
notes: Pro-Russian hacktivist collective conducting DDoS attacks against NATO member governments, hospitals, and financial institutions since early 2022 Russia-Ukraine invasion; recruits via Telegram.

name: Kimsuky
notes: North Korean RGB intelligence-collection unit targeting South Korean think tanks, nuclear operators, policy institutions, and Western governments with social engineering and spear-phishing.

name: LAPSUS$
notes: Loosely organized extortion group (primarily UK/Brazilian teen hackers) that breached Microsoft, Okta, Nvidia, Samsung, and Uber via SIM-swapping, social engineering, and insider recruitment; multiple members arrested.

name: Lazarus Group
notes: North Korean Reconnaissance General Bureau umbrella group responsible for $3B+ in cryptocurrency theft, the Sony Pictures hack, WannaCry ransomware, and ongoing supply chain attacks against crypto and DeFi platforms.

name: LockBit
notes: World's most prolific RaaS operation 2021-2024 with 2,000+ known victims; disrupted by Operation Cronos (Feb 2024) but resumed operations; administrator Dmitry Khoroshev indicted by US DOJ.

name: Lynx
notes: Emerged in 2024 with code similarities to INC Ransom; rapidly grew to top-10 status in 2025 with 161+ attacks; targets broad sectors including utilities, retail, and professional services.

name: Magic Hound
notes: Iranian IRGC-linked group conducting long-running spear-phishing and social engineering campaigns against academics, policy researchers, journalists, and Jewish/Israeli individuals globally.

name: Medusa
notes: RaaS operation active since 2021 that surged into top-10 status in 2024-2025; uses Telegram and a public blog for victim pressure; targets healthcare, education, and government globally.

name: menuPass
notes: MSS-linked group conducting Cloud Hopper managed service provider (MSP) compromise campaigns against Western defense, healthcare, finance, and government clients through supply chain pivot.

name: Money Message
notes: RaaS operation emerging in early 2023 demanding multi-million dollar ransoms and deploying a cross-platform Go encryptor; claimed attacks on PC maker MSI and Philippine Airlines.

name: Moonstone Sleet
notes: North Korean group mixing espionage and ransomware using elaborate fake companies, malicious npm packages, and custom malware; targets defense contractors and software developers.

name: MuddyWater
notes: Iranian MOIS-affiliated group conducting espionage and destructive attacks against Middle Eastern governments, telecoms, and defense organizations; uses legitimate RMM tools (Atera, SimpleHelp) as C2.

name: Mustang Panda
notes: PRC-linked group conducting prolific spear-phishing campaigns using PlugX malware against European governments, Vatican, NGOs, and Southeast Asian government targets.

name: Mustard Tempest
notes: Initial access broker operating SocGholish/FakeUpdates malware distribution network; sells access to Evil Corp and other ransomware affiliates via drive-by download campaigns on compromised websites.

name: NoName057(16)
notes: Pro-Russian hacktivist group conducting coordinated DDoS attacks against NATO government, financial, and transportation targets since March 2022 using DDoSia volunteer botnet toolkit.

name: NSO Group
notes: Israeli mercenary spyware company whose Pegasus platform uses zero-click exploits to surveil journalists, activists, and heads of state on behalf of government clients; US Commerce Dept entity-listed November 2021.

name: OilRig
notes: Iranian MOIS-linked group (alias-shared with APT34 designation) targeting Middle East governments, energy, and financial institutions with DNS tunneling and custom web shells for persistent access.

name: Play
notes: RaaS group active since 2022 deploying PlayCrypt ransomware; notable for targeting VMware ESXi, exploiting Fortinet and RDP vulnerabilities; 300+ victims in North America, Latin America, and Europe.

name: Predatory Sparrow
notes: Anti-Iran group (possibly Israeli-linked) responsible for destructive attacks on Iranian steel plants (2022), gas stations (2023), and rail infrastructure; uses custom wiper malware.

name: Qilin
notes: Top-ranked RaaS group in 2025 with 946+ disclosed victims; targets VMware ESXi and Windows with Rust-based ransomware; absorbed many RansomHub affiliates after April 2025 outage.

name: Quadream
notes: Israeli mercenary spyware vendor selling Reign zero-click iPhone surveillance platform; shut down operations in May 2023 after Citizen Lab/Microsoft exposure of exploitation of journalists and opposition figures.

name: RansomHub
notes: Former top-ranked RaaS (736 victims in 2024) that went dark in April 2025; largely absorbed into DragonForce cartel infrastructure; recruited heavily from disbanded LockBit and ALPHV affiliates.

name: REvil
notes: Russian RaaS responsible for Kaseya VSA supply chain ransomware attack (1,500 organizations), JBS Foods ($11M ransom), and Quanta Computer/Apple extortion; dismantled by Russian FSB arrests in January 2022.

name: Rhysida
notes: RaaS group launched mid-2023 with strong operational ties to Vice Society; targets healthcare, education, and government with double extortion; responsible for Prospect Medical, British Library, and Army of Chile breaches.

name: Salt Typhoon
notes: Chinese MSS-linked group responsible for the most significant telecom espionage campaign in US history (2024-2025), breaching AT&T, Verizon, T-Mobile, and nine other carriers to intercept government communications and CALEA surveillance data.

name: Sandworm Team
notes: GRU Unit 74455 conducting the most destructive cyberattacks in history including Ukraine power grid attacks (2015-2016), NotPetya ($10B+ damage), and ongoing wiper/sabotage campaigns against Ukrainian infrastructure.

name: Scattered Spider
notes: English-speaking (US/UK) social engineering collective known for help-desk impersonation, SIM swapping, and MFA bypass targeting cloud identity platforms; responsible for MGM Resorts, Caesars, and Coinbase breaches.

name: Sea Turtle
notes: Turkish state-linked group conducting DNS hijacking and supply chain attacks against Kurdish entities, telecom providers, and ISPs in the Middle East and North Africa.

name: ShinyHunters
notes: Data theft and extortion group responsible for breaches at Snowflake customer environments (2024), Ticketmaster, AT&T, and 70+ other organizations; operated BreachForums before IntelBroker takeover.

name: Silence Group
notes: Russian-speaking financially motivated group targeting banks in Eastern Europe and Asia using customized Delphi-based tools; known for conducting reconnaissance over weeks before launching card fraud operations.

name: Star Blizzard
notes: FSB Centre 18-linked group conducting spear-phishing and credential theft campaigns against NATO governments, NGOs, journalists, and former intelligence officers; disrupted by Microsoft/DOJ domain seizures in 2024.

name: Storm-0501
notes: Ransomware affiliate and access broker that pivots from initial access to multi-stage attacks, deploying Embargo ransomware; known for targeting US hospitals and critical infrastructure.

name: Storm-1811
notes: Threat actor conducting vishing attacks impersonating IT help desks via Microsoft Teams to deploy Black Basta ransomware; exploits legitimate remote management tools for lateral movement.

name: TA505
notes: Prolific Russian-speaking group operating Cl0p ransomware and historically the Dridex botnet; responsible for mass zero-day exploitation campaigns targeting file transfer software affecting thousands of organizations.

name: Threat Group-3390
notes: Chinese MSS-affiliated group conducting long-dwell espionage against defense contractors, government agencies, and financial institutions; known for HyperBro RAT and SysUpdate backdoor.

name: ToddyCat
notes: China-linked group targeting European and Asian government and defense organizations with Ninja backdoor and Samurai passive backdoor via Exchange server exploitation.

name: Transparent Tribe
notes: Pakistan-linked group conducting espionage against Indian military, government, and education targets using CrimsonRAT and ObliqueRAT delivered via phishing and watering holes.

name: Trigona
notes: RaaS operation active 2022-2023 targeting healthcare, finance, and legal sectors; infrastructure seized by Ukrainian police in October 2023; associated CNC servers and forums taken offline.

name: Turla
notes: FSB-linked elite espionage group active for 25+ years targeting diplomatic, government, and military organizations globally using highly sophisticated Snake/Uroburos implant network; dismantled by FBI in 2023.

name: UNC3886
notes: Chinese espionage actor specializing in compromising VMware ESXi hypervisors and network edge devices (Fortinet, Ivanti) to deploy persistent backdoors in hard-to-scan environments.

name: Velvet Ant
notes: China-nexus threat actor conducting multi-year stealthy persistence in large organization networks using legacy on-premises infrastructure and F5 BIG-IP exploits; discovered maintaining 3-year footholds.

name: Vice Society
notes: Ransomware group active 2021-2023 that disproportionately targeted education and healthcare sectors; evolved into Rhysida ransomware operation; known for deploying HelloKitty and Zeppelin variants.

name: Volt Typhoon
notes: PRC-linked group pre-positioning on US critical infrastructure (energy, water, communications, transportation) for potential disruptive operations; uses living-off-the-land techniques to avoid detection.

name: Winter Vivern
notes: Russia/Belarus-aligned group targeting European government email portals and NATO think tanks; exploits Zimbra and Roundcube webmail vulnerabilities to steal diplomatic communications.

name: Wizard Spider
notes: Russian cybercriminal organization responsible for TrickBot, Ryuk, and Conti ransomware operations; estimated $2.7B in damages; evolved into Black Basta operations after Conti shutdown.
